meaning: adj.秘密的,含义模糊的,神秘的,隐藏的
examples: He has issued a short, cryptic statement denying the spying charges.
Other ways an organism may be cryptic include nocturnality, subterranean lifestyle, and transparency.
Leave cryptic messages on the typewriters.
Cryptic thereby defies a troubling trend: Internet games, first ballyhooed as the next big thing, have struggled.
But such losses will probably be made up as DNA evidence reveals overlooked "cryptic" species.
